name: PyAudio (Python Audio I/O Library)

agent_readiness.notes: Python bindings for PortAudio for audio recording and playback. import pyaudio; import wave. p = pyaudio.PyAudio(). Recording: stream = p.open(format=pyaudio.paInt16, channels=1, rate=44100, input=True, frames_per_buffer=1024). frames = [stream.read(1024) for _ in range(int(44100/1024 * 5))]. Playback: stream = p.open(format, channels, rate, output=True). stream.write(data). Callback mode: p.open(stream_callback=callback). wf = wave.open('audio.wav', 'rb'). Device enumeration: p.get_device_info_by_index(i). p.terminate(). Installs PortAudio. Used for microphone input (voice apps, STT). Often combined with faster-whisper or openai Whisper. sounddevice is a more Pythonic alternative.

gotchas: Installation requires PortAudio system library (brew, apt-get)
Blocking stream.read() must match buffer size exactly
macOS microphone permission required — may silently fail
Callback mode is complex to implement correctly
sounddevice is more Pythonic with numpy array support
best_when: Audio playback in Python desktop applications
Real-time audio streaming for speech-to-text pipelines
When PortAudio compatibility with specific hardware is needed
avoid_when: Audio file processing (use pydub or soundfile)
When sounddevice's numpy interface is cleaner
summary: PyAudio provides Python bindings for PortAudio audio I/O. p = pyaudio.PyAudio(); stream = p.open(format, channels, rate, input=True); data = stream.read(1024). Microphone recording, audio playback, device enumeration. MIT. Requires PortAudio system lib (brew/apt). sounddevice is more Pythonic with numpy support. Best for microphone input in voice/STT apps. Use pydub for audio file manipulation.
